user_query:
Perform a read-only security audit of my OpenClaw/Clawdbot deployment using the provided snapshots under input/. Do not run any system commands; instead, treat the files as captured outputs/configs. Produce two deliverables:

1) Write a terminal-formatted audit report to output/openclaw_audit_report.txt following this structure exactly:
OPENCLAW SECURITY AUDIT REPORT
Host: <hostname>  OS: <os>  Kernel: <kernel>
Gateway: <status + version if available>
Timestamp: <UTC>

[CHECK ID] <Title>
Status: OK | VULNERABLE | UNKNOWN
Evidence: <brief quotes from input/ files that support the verdict>
Impact: <why it matters>
Fix: <specific steps>

...repeat for each check...

SUMMARY
Total checks: <n>
OK: <n>  VULNERABLE: <n>  UNKNOWN: <n>
Top 3 Risks:
- <risk 1>
- <risk 2>
- <risk 3>

Run these checks using only the input/ files:
- ENV-IDENT: Identify OS, kernel, hostname (input/os-release.txt, input/uname.txt, input/hostname.txt)
- OPENCLAW-VERSION: Detect OpenClaw presence and version (input/version.txt)
- NET-EXPOSE: Network exposure and listening services (input/ss.txt)
- GW-AUTH: Gateway bind and authentication configuration (input/openclaw_config.json)
- UI-CSWSH: Control UI token/CSWSH risk (assume versions < 1.3.0 are vulnerable; use input/version.txt, input/logs.txt, input/openclaw_config.json)
- TOOL-POLICY: Tool & exec policy review (input/openclaw_config.json, input/tool_policy.json)
- SKILL-SUPPLY: Skills & supply-chain risk (input/skills.json)
- SECRET-STORAGE: Credentials & secret storage (input/secrets_listing.txt)
- FILE-PERMS: File permissions & privilege escalation risks (input/permissions.txt)
- PERSISTENCE: Process & persistence indicators (input/cron.txt, input/systemd.txt)
- LOGS: Logs & audit trails (input/logs.txt)

Mark items VULNERABLE, OK, or UNKNOWN based on the evidence. Redact any secrets; report only file paths and metadata. Include concrete evidence strings in the Evidence lines (e.g., ‘0.0.0.0:18789’, ‘auth mode: none’, ‘gatewayUrl’, ‘curl | bash’, ‘tokens.json 0644’, ‘drwxrwxrwx’, ‘unknown-skill-updater’, ‘203.0.113.5’). For UI-CSWSH, treat version 1.2.3 as vulnerable.

2) Write a machine-readable summary to output/summary.json with keys: {"totalChecks": <int>, "ok": <int>, "vulnerable": <int>, "unknown": <int>, "topRisks": [<3+ strings>]}

Files to use are under input/: os-release.txt, uname.txt, hostname.txt, version.txt, ss.txt, openclaw_config.json, tool_policy.json, skills.json, secrets_listing.txt, permissions.txt, cron.txt, systemd.txt, logs.txt.
